summary: Built revocation-with-provenance as a real feature (PRD §6.5: revocation is detection, not prevention — only as fast as a contact re-resolves, and only safe from witness-corroborated state, never a relay's cache). New crates/murmur-core/src/corroboration.rs pairs a resolved DelegationState with its Provenance (WitnessCorroborated{confirmed,threshold} | RelayCache{revocations_behind_witnesses}); provenance, not contents, decides corroboration, so a relay cache is never corroborated even when it happens to be current. CorroboratedState::resolve_revocation rejects a revoked device from witness-corroborated state (RevocationResolution::RevokedFromCorroboratedState) and DISCLOSES the honest stale-served window for a relay cache (StaleWindowDisclosed{cache_shows_revoked, revocations_behind_witnesses}) — a sub-threshold witness set or a live device fails closed, and a cache is never laundered into a corroborated clawback. lib.rs prove_revocation_corroborated drives both halves hermetically: the root anchors then revokes a delegated device; a contact re-resolving the witness-corroborated set rejects it, and a contact served the pre-revocation snapshot (the stale cache) is told the window — failing closed on a revoked device accepted from corroborated state, a relay cache trusted over the witnesses, or a hidden stale window. murmur-relay serve gains an 11th leg (run_revocation_corroborated) emitting the revoked-from-corroborated-state + stale-window-disclosed markers the probe greps for. No crypto reimplemented; built on the existing delegation/anchor/revocation signature primitives. Probe RED→GREEN; matrix --gate GREEN fleet-wide (0 regressions/broken/stale, 12/12 traps RED, leakcheck clean over both trees, app sculpt gate OK); 85 murmur-core/relay unit tests pass; clippy clean. The honest boundary is preserved: a corroborated rejection is the only safe outcome, a relay cache is always disclosed as a window — never oversold as an instant global kill.
